question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY environment variable inject an arbitrary private key so that submitWithRetry() calls Execute(client) and signs a CryptoTransfer on behalf of an account they do not own, authorizing fund movement without the true account owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded default operatorKey value ('302e020100300506032b657004220420...') in config.go to submit a CryptoTransfer via Execute(client) against account 0.0.2 without any approval from the legitimate key holder, because the default key is publicly known?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID to any account ID cause submitWithRetry() to debit that account via AddHbarTransfer(client.GetOperatorAccountID(), ...) without the real account owner ever approving the transaction, because no ownership proof beyond key possession is verified?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that NewTransferTransaction().Execute(client) signs the transaction solely with the operator key set via SetOperator(), and exploit the absence of any multi-signature or threshold-key enforcement to authorize a transfer that should require M-of-N signers, bypassing co-signer approval entirely?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who gains read access to the process environment (e.g., via /proc/self/environ in a shared container) extract the plaintext HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY and replay it in their own Execute(client) call to authorize transfers without the owner's knowledge or consent?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to their own account ID so that AddHbarTransfer(toID, hiero.HbarFromTinybar(cfg.amountTinybar)) credits them, causing the operator's funds to be transferred to an attacker-controlled account without the operator ever explicitly approving that specific destination?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to a large positive integer so that AddHbarTransfer(client.GetOperatorAccountID(), hiero.HbarFromTinybar(-cfg.amountTinybar)) drains the operator account in a single Execute(client) call, with no per-transfer approval gate checking whether the operator consented to that specific amount?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to a negative value (e.g., -1) so that the sign of AddHbarTransfer(client.GetOperatorAccountID(), hiero.HbarFromTinybar(-cfg.amountTinybar)) flips, crediting the operator account and debiting toID without toID's owner approving the outgoing transfer?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Execute(client) submits the transaction without checking whether the destination account (toID) has receiverSigRequired=true, thereby bypassing the receiver's mandatory approval and completing a transfer the receiver never authorized?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cfg.toAccountID set it equal to cfg.operatorID to trigger a self-transfer loop via AddHbarTransfer, consuming network fees on every ticker tick without any owner approval for each individual fee-burning transaction?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any transaction memo or unique nonce in NewTransferTransaction() to replay a previously observed transaction ID, causing Execute(client) to re-authorize a transfer the operator did not intend to repeat?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to INT_MAX, causing submitWithRetry() to loop indefinitely calling Execute(client) and authorizing repeated transfers without any per-attempt owner re-approval?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that resp.GetReceipt(client) failure in submitWithRetry() does not roll back or void the already-submitted transaction, meaning Execute(client) may have already authorized and settled a transfer even when the function ultimately returns an error?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a race condition by sending SIGTERM between Execute(client) and resp.GetReceipt(client) in submitWithRetry(), leaving a submitted-but-unconfirmed transfer authorized on-chain without the operator being aware the transaction was accepted?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the context cancellation path in submitWithRetry() — where ctx.Done() is checked only at the top of the loop — to ensure Execute(client) is called at least once even after cancellation is requested, authorizing a transfer the operator intended to abort?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK environment variable set it to 'other' and supply a malicious HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST URL, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to return attacker-controlled node endpoints so that Execute(client) submits the signed transaction to a rogue node that records the operator's signature without authorization?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS certificate validation in the http.Client used by fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to perform a man-in-the-middle attack, injecting rogue node addresses so that Execute(client) sends the signed CryptoTransfer to an attacker-controlled endpoint, leaking the operator's authorization signature?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to '1ns' so that the ticker fires at maximum rate, causing submitWithRetry() to call Execute(client) thousands of times per second and authorize thousands of transfers without any per-transfer owner approval, draining the operator account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NewTransferTransaction() does not set an explicit transaction valid duration or expiry, allowing Execute(client) to authorize a transfer with the network's maximum validity window, giving an attacker a large time window to replay or front-run the signed transaction?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who can read log output extract the txID from the log.Printf('transfer success: ... txID=%s ...') line and use it to query the network for the full signed transaction bytes, then reuse the operator's authorization signature in a crafted transaction?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any allowance/approval check before Execute(client) to transfer funds from the operator account without the operator having explicitly granted a spending allowance for that specific amount and destination, violating HIP-336 approval semantics?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cfg.operatorKey supply a key that matches a multi-sig account's single sub-key, causing Execute(client) to submit a partial-signature transaction that the network accepts if the threshold is 1, authorizing a transfer without the other co-signers' approval?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that submitWithRetry() never verifies that client.GetOperatorAccountID() actually owns the funds being debited via AddHbarTransfer(..., hiero.HbarFromTinybar(-cfg.amountTinybar)), allowing any account ID to be set as operator and have transfers authorized against it using only a key string?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the missing freeze-status check in submitWithRetry() to attempt Execute(client) on a frozen account, and if the SDK silently ignores the freeze, authorize a transfer that the account owner intended to block via the freeze mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the missing KYC-status check in submitWithRetry() to call Execute(client) for a token transfer on an account that has not completed KYC, bypassing the KYC approval requirement that the token issuer (owner) mandated?
